(Romanian: [[stadiˈonul reˈpublit͡ʃij]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian"), *Stadium of the Republic*) was a multi-use [stadium](/wiki/Stadium "Stadium") in [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania").

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [44°25′42″N 26°4′55″E﻿ / ﻿44.42833°N 26.08194°E﻿ / 44.42833; 26.08194](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Stadionul_Republicii&params=44_25_42_N_26_4_55_E_region:RO_type:landmark_scale:10000)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former Romanian stadium

For stadium in Chișinău, see [Stadionul Republican](/wiki/Stadionul_Republican "Stadionul Republican").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/Stadionul_Republicii_in_the_early_80%27s.jpg/330px-Stadionul_Republicii_in_the_early_80%27s.jpg)](/wiki/File:Stadionul_Republicii_in_the_early_80%27s.jpg)

*Stadionul Republicii* in the early 1980s

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Bundesarchiv_Bild_183-17140-0001%2C_Bukarest%2C_Fu%C3%9Fballspiel_Rum%C3%A4nien-DDR.jpg/330px-Bundesarchiv_Bild_183-17140-0001%2C_Bukarest%2C_Fu%C3%9Fballspiel_Rum%C3%A4nien-DDR.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bundesarchiv_Bild_183-17140-0001,_Bukarest,_Fu%C3%9Fballspiel_Rum%C3%A4nien-DDR.jpg)

A friendly football match at *Stadionul Republicii* between [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team") and [East Germany](/wiki/East_Germany_national_football_team "East Germany national football team"), 26 October 1952

**Stadionul Republicii** (Romanian: [[stadiˈonul reˈpublit͡ʃij]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian"), *Stadium of the Republic*) was a multi-use [stadium](/wiki/Stadium "Stadium") in [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest"), [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania").

It was used mostly for [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") matches. The stadium was able to hold 28,000 spectators and originally opened in 1926. It hosted 42 matches for the [Romania national football team](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), 1 match for the Olympic National Team, 22 Romanian Football Cup finals and 15 European Cup matches.

The stadium was originally built in 1926 as the **Stadionul Oficiul Național de Educație Fizică**, or simply **Stadionul ONEF**, and inaugurated on 9 May 1926 with a rugby match against the French army. It was destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the name Stadionul Republicii in 1948.

It was located on [Spirii Hill](/wiki/Spirii_Hill "Spirii Hill"), the current location of the [Palace of the Parliament](/wiki/Palace_of_the_Parliament "Palace of the Parliament"). Since everything on the hill was cleared, including a former social high class and historic neighborhood, the stadium was demolished.

There were projects to move the National Football Stadium that would host a [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") final to the same location, but those failed when the city's town hall decided to demolish the [Stadionul Național](/wiki/Stadionul_Na%C8%9Bional_(1953) "Stadionul Național (1953)") and build a new one.
